name: How does AEO software actually work day-to-day, and what would my team be doing inside it each week?

mainEntity.name: How does AEO software actually work day-to-day, and what would my team be doing inside it each week?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You typically use AEO software by connecting it to your domains and key topics, letting it run queries across major answer engines, then reviewing dashboards that show where you’re cited, missing, or misrepresented. The software then guides changes to content structure, schema markup, and authority signals so AI systems are more likely to pull your pages into answers.

Most platforms combine crawler-style scans of your site with automated prompts to AI models, logging whether and how your brand appears in responses. From there, you iterate: adjust content, re-test, and track visibility improvements.

Key factors:
- Initial setup (domains, brand names, priority topics, competitors)
- Frequency of answer-engine scans and prompt runs
- Supported technical checks (schema, robots, page structure)
- Workflow features (tasks, annotations, change logs)

In practice, plan to use AEO software weekly: review changes in citations, prioritize a few pages or topics, make content updates, and re-measure. Treat it as an ongoing optimization loop, similar to SEO—but tuned for AI answers.

Position AEO tools as part of your core search and content operations, not a one-off project you “set and forget.”
acceptedAnswer.description: Day-to-day, AEO software runs structured queries across major answer engines, logs whether your brand is cited, and surfaces technical and content issues that block visibility. Your team then updates content and schema based on those insights, re-tests, and tracks AI citation gains in weekly or monthly dashboards.
